Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibehigashi 8-4-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikafukitsu-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ueda Daimyōjin-sha (old name) |札所等 = |例祭 = Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshihime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshihime-jinja, Haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Kagura hall]] [[ファイル:Yoshihime-jinja, Honden.jpg|サムネイル|Honden]] '''Yoshihime Shrine''' (よしひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === There is a spring in front of the shrine said to be effective for all illnesses[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/8353 Yoshihime Shrine - Shiga/Biwako Tourism Information] accessed 2018-03-27. The wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are also said to have been made in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It forms a paired relationship with Yoshimiko Shrine, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === History === Founding is unknown, but like {{ill|lt=Yoshimiko Shrine|Q11413129|WD=Q11413129|1=Yoshimiko Shrine}}, it is regarded as a descendant shrine of Ishibe Kashioe Shrine, a Shikinaisha. Originally it stood at the current {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} location, but it burned in warfare during the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era and is said to have moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=1534|Q6447|WD=Q6447|1=1534}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Ueda Daimyōjin-sha, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 it received official approval to change to the current shrine name, and in September of the same year, during {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231|1=Emperor Meiji}}'s {{ill|lt=Tōkyō procession|Q11235215|WD=Q11235215|lt=eastward progress|1=Emperor Meiji's visits to Tokyo}}, Jingikan official {{ill|lt=Uematsu Masakoto|Q22128646|WD=Q22128646|1=Uematsu Masakoto}} was sent as envoy and offerings were granted. ===Enshrined deities=== ; Main deity *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Associated deities * Kamikafukitsu-hime * Yoshihime Ōkami === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibehigashi 8-4-1 === Access === === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Konan City Community Bus|Q11563211|WD=Q11563211|1=Konan City Community Bus}}, get off at Ishibe Kōban-mae stop, then 7 minutes on foot. * 30 minutes on foot from the same station. === By car === * About 20 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. Parking available. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=518 Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association)] * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/8353/ Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga/Biwako Tourism Information)] * [https://yaokami.jp/1250967/ Yoshihime Shrine (Yaokami)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/shaji/yoshihime-jinja/ Yoshihime Shrine (Konan City Tourism Guide)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/event/post_79/ Ishibe Grand Festival (Konan City Tourism Guide)] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:湖南市の歴史]] [[Category:湖南市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]]

This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === History === Founding is unknown, but like {{ill|lt=Yoshimiko Shrine|Q11413129|WD=Q11413129|1=Yoshimiko Shrine}}, it is regarded as a descendant shrine of Ishibe Kashioe Shrine, a Shikinaisha. Originally it stood at the current {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} location, but it burned in warfare during the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era and is said to have moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=1534|Q6447|WD=Q6447|1=1534}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Ueda Daimyōjin-sha, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 it received official approval to change to the current shrine name, and in September of the same year, during {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231|1=Emperor Meiji}}'s {{ill|lt=Tōkyō procession|Q11235215|WD=Q11235215|lt=eastward progress|1=Emperor Meiji's visits to Tokyo}}, Jingikan official {{ill|lt=Uematsu Masakoto|Q22128646|WD=Q22128646|1=Uematsu Masakoto}} was sent as envoy and offerings were granted. ===Enshrined deities=== ; Main deity *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Associated deities * Kamikafukitsu-hime * Yoshihime Ōkami === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibehigashi 8-4-1 === Access === === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Konan City Community Bus|Q11563211|WD=Q11563211|1=Konan City Community Bus}}, get off at Ishibe Kōban-mae stop, then 7 minutes on foot. * 30 minutes on foot from the same station. === By car === * About 20 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. Parking available. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=518 Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association)] * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/8353/ Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga/Biwako Tourism Information)] * [https://yaokami.jp/1250967/ Yoshihime Shrine (Yaokami)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/shaji/yoshihime-jinja/ Yoshihime Shrine (Konan City Tourism Guide)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/event/post_79/ Ishibe Grand Festival (Konan City Tourism Guide)] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:湖南市の歴史]] [[Category:湖南市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikafukitsu-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ueda Daimyōjin-sha (old name) |札所等 = |例祭 = Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshihime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshihime-jinja, Haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Kagura hall]] [[ファイル:Yoshihime-jinja, Honden.jpg|サムネイル|Honden]] '''Yoshihime Shrine''' (よしひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === There is a spring in front of the shrine said to be effective for all illnesses[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/8353 Yoshihime Shrine - Shiga/Biwako Tourism Information] accessed 2018-03-27. The wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are also said to have been made in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It forms a paired relationship with Yoshimiko Shrine, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === History === Founding is unknown, but like {{ill|lt=Yoshimiko Shrine|Q11413129|WD=Q11413129|1=Yoshimiko Shrine}}, it is regarded as a descendant shrine of Ishibe Kashioe Shrine, a Shikinaisha. Originally it stood at the current {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} location, but it burned in warfare during the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era and is said to have moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=1534|Q6447|WD=Q6447|1=1534}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Ueda Daimyōjin-sha, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 it received official approval to change to the current shrine name, and in September of the same year, during {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231|1=Emperor Meiji}}'s {{ill|lt=Tōkyō procession|Q11235215|WD=Q11235215|lt=eastward progress|1=Emperor Meiji's visits to Tokyo}}, Jingikan official {{ill|lt=Uematsu Masakoto|Q22128646|WD=Q22128646|1=Uematsu Masakoto}} was sent as envoy and offerings were granted. ===Enshrined deities=== ; Main deity *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Associated deities * Kamikafukitsu-hime * Yoshihime Ōkami === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibehigashi 8-4-1 === Access === === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Konan City Community Bus|Q11563211|WD=Q11563211|1=Konan City Community Bus}}, get off at Ishibe Kōban-mae stop, then 7 minutes on foot. * 30 minutes on foot from the same station. === By car === * About 20 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. Parking available. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=518 Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association)] * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/8353/ Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga/Biwako Tourism Information)] * [https://yaokami.jp/1250967/ Yoshihime Shrine (Yaokami)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/shaji/yoshihime-jinja/ Yoshihime Shrine (Konan City Tourism Guide)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/event/post_79/ Ishibe Grand Festival (Konan City Tourism Guide)] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:湖南市の歴史]] [[Category:湖南市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]]

Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibehigashi 8-4-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshihime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ueda Daimyōjin-sha (old name) |札所等 = |例祭 = Ishibe Festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yoshihime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Yoshihime-jinja, Haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Kagura hall]] [[ファイル:Yoshihime-jinja, Honden.jpg|サムネイル|Honden]] '''Yoshihime Shrine''' (よしひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === There is a spring in front of the shrine said to be effective for all illnesses[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/8353 Yoshihime Shrine - Shiga/Biwako Tourism Information] accessed 2018-03-27. The wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are also said to have been made in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival: May 1 : * It forms a paired relationship with Yoshimiko Shrine, and mikoshi from both shrines come out on that day. This is called the "Ishibe Grand Festival." === History === Founding is unknown, but like {{ill|lt=Yoshimiko Shrine|Q11413129|WD=Q11413129|1=Yoshimiko Shrine}}, it is regarded as a descendant shrine of Ishibe Kashioe Shrine, a Shikinaisha. Originally it stood at the current {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} location, but it burned in warfare during the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era and is said to have moved to the current site in {{ill|lt=1534|Q6447|WD=Q6447|1=1534}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 3). In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Ueda Daimyōjin-sha, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 it received official approval to change to the current shrine name, and in September of the same year, during {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231|1=Emperor Meiji}}'s {{ill|lt=Tōkyō procession|Q11235215|WD=Q11235215|lt=eastward progress|1=Emperor Meiji's visits to Tokyo}}, Jingikan official {{ill|lt=Uematsu Masakoto|Q22128646|WD=Q22128646|1=Uematsu Masakoto}} was sent as envoy and offerings were granted. ===Enshrined deities=== ; Main deity *{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Associated deities * Kamikafukitsu-hime * Yoshihime Ōkami === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Konan|Q857194|WD=Q857194|1=Konan, Shiga}}Ishibehigashi 8-4-1 === Access === === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Ishibe Station|Q5362466|WD=Q5362466|1=Ishibe Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Kusatsu Line|Q865516|WD=Q865516|1=Kusatsu Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Konan City Community Bus|Q11563211|WD=Q11563211|1=Konan City Community Bus}}, get off at Ishibe Kōban-mae stop, then 7 minutes on foot. * 30 minutes on foot from the same station. === By car === * About 20 minutes from {{ill|lt=Rittō Interchange|Q11110683|WD=Q11110683|lt=Rittō IC|1=Ritto interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|1=Meishin Expressway}}. Parking available. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=518 Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association)] * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/8353/ Yoshihime Shrine (Shiga/Biwako Tourism Information)] * [https://yaokami.jp/1250967/ Yoshihime Shrine (Yaokami)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/shaji/yoshihime-jinja/ Yoshihime Shrine (Konan City Tourism Guide)] * [https://www.burari-konan.jp/kanko/event/post_79/ Ishibe Grand Festival (Konan City Tourism Guide)] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yoshihime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:湖南市の歴史]] [[Category:湖南市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]]
